(i) the short-timeframe between when the reduction to 50% to now (only 1 month) does not, in our opinion, enable an appropriate time to really analyze and assess the effectiveness of the response (slashing rewards) to the stated problem.  1 month is simply too short a time to really understand if it is being effective/addressing the issue; (ii) it does not appear that there is a long-term/strategic centralization plan for Compound.  This sentiment was brought up in the forum discussions - whereby, the commenters basically argued that a much higher-level/strategic view needed to be done on a systemic basis, with more stakeholders, to really take these kinds of major decisions regarding how to best approach decentralization, including this issue of rewards, at a more strategic/thoughtful level.  Further, it was noted that there were not enough attempt by the proposer to get more stakeholders to comment/get involved in the proposal/addressing the issue. Basically, a few commenters indicated that hard changes can/should be attempted - but they should be done in a strategic way, and involve more stakeholders than had occurred in this process.

CP064 has a fix for the exploit that has minimal negative impact and is a clear win for the protocol. CP063 is a less elegant fix that will temporarily break integrations. This was not an easy decision, and we'll provide more information on our reasoning once the change is queued and executed (or cancelled). We cannot comment with any further detail as this is an ongoing exploit.

It will likely take time for Compound III markets to take off, but now is the time to set the stage
Although the system worked as intended at the time, the design has been improved and we have the reserves to make this right for affected users.

The Compound community values on-chain governance, but most users who supported the protocol before liquidity mining have no meaningful voice in on-chain governance as their capital could not compete with deep-pocketed professionals entering our markets to earn COMP. This initiative supports the health of the protocol and community.

The purpose of COMP governance token is to empower users with governance over the protocol, but more than 9 in 10 COMP distributed to users is being claimed and immediately sold, usually through automated yield farming strategies. The proposed adjustments will allow governance to better align the future distribution of governance rights to parties engaged in healthy, sustainable stewardship of the protocol.
